author: y4rr
content:
Plan: Discovered during Phase 3 (#996) value-parity probe of ViT-B/16. The Phase 3 pre-flight expected ViT to PASS torch.allclose (vit.rs:1-13 declares 'All operations use differentiable primitives from ferrotorch_core' with no architectural-divergence note). Strict value-parity loader actually REPORTS — torchvision vit_b_16 state_dict uses schema:
  encoder.layers.encoder_layer_<N>.ln_1.weight
  encoder.layers.encoder_layer_<N>.ln_2.weight
  encoder.layers.encoder_layer_<N>.self_attention.in_proj_weight
  encoder.layers.encoder_layer_<N>.mlp.0.weight  (Linear)
  encoder.layers.encoder_layer_<N>.mlp.3.weight  (Linear after activation+dropout)
  heads.head.weight
  class_token / encoder.pos_embedding / conv_proj.*

The ferrotorch impl exposes (per ferrotorch-vision/src/models/vit.rs:479-498):
  blocks.<N>.norm1.weight
  blocks.<N>.norm2.weight
  blocks.<N>.attn.* (MultiheadAttention's schema)
  blocks.<N>.mlp.fc1.weight
  blocks.<N>.mlp.fc2.weight
  head.weight
  cls_token / pos_embed / patch_embed.proj.*

This is a Phase-1B-style 'named_parameters cannot match torchvision state_dict keys' divergence — the math may agree but the loader cannot adopt torchvision weights without renaming. NOT a Phase 3 scope item (Phase 3 only migrated permute-pattern CPU pulls; the migration is correct and the existing model-level tests stay green). Surfaced under failure mode #19 'Architectural-completion phase needed for vision models'.

Out of scope for #996. Architect concern: schema-rename phase is needed (analogous to Phase 2's BN-buffer alignment) before ViT can pass torch.allclose. Tests are #[ignore = '#999 ...']'d under #996 with the issue number cited per the pre-flight.
